Title: Metabolic engineering of proanthocyanidin production by repressing the isoflavone pathways and redirecting anthocyanidin precursor flux in legume

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression positively correlated with increased PA accumulation in MtPAR hairy roots
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT72L1 was upregulated in MtPAR-expressing roots. 2. Its expression correlated with PA accumulation, supporting PA pathway activation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in MtPAR hairy roots with high PA
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for upstream MtPAR transgene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PA-specific glycosyltransferase discussed as downstream target rather than primary manipulation target

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Upregulated with MtPAR and associated with increased PA accumulation/transport capacity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtMATE1 expression correlated positively with MtPAR and PA accumulation. 2. Differential upregulation of MtMATE1 supports transport/sequestration of PA precursors/products during MtPAR-mediated engineering.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for upstream MtPAR transgene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as epicatechin 3'-O-glucoside transporter in discussion

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression was not notably affected by MtPAR, consistent with MtPAR selectively favoring PA over anthocyanin transport branch
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtMATE2, an anthocyanin transporter gene, did not show obvious induction by MtPAR. 2. This supported pathway specificity of MtPAR toward PA rather than anthocyanin branch regulation.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No major change in MtPAR hairy roots
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for upstream MtPAR transgene
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because differential transporter regulation was part of the mechanistic model

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces anthocyanidins to flavan-3-ols such as epicatechin for PA formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ANR expression is associated with increased PA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR correlation; promoter transactivation assay by MtPAR+MtTT8+MtWD40-1; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANR expression positively correlated with PA accumulation in MtPAR-expressing hairy roots. 2. MtPAR together with MtTT8 and MtWD40-1 activated the ANR promoter about ninefold. 3. Increased ANR expression accompanied enhanced PA production in Medicago and alfalfa systems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MtPAR-expressing hairy roots and further increased in MtPAR+MtLAP1 alfalfa hairy roots/plants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transgenic overexpression of MtPAR and/or MtLAP1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forage quality trait associated with bloat protection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with anthocyanin branch for anthocyanidin precursors
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene was not itself the primary manipulation target but was a direct downstream activated PA structural gene.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, ArrayExpress E-MEXP-3485; metabolomics by UPLC-ESI-qTOF-MS/LC-MS-MS

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones to 2-hydroxyisoflavanones, committing flux into the isoflavone branch
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts flux away from PA toward isoflavone branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MtPAR directly represses IFS promoter activity/expression, reducing isoflavone accumulation and redirecting flux toward PA-related flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling; yeast one-hybrid promoter binding to MtIFS1 and GmIFS2 promoters
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IFS expression was downregulated in MtPAR-expressing Medicago and soybean hairy roots. 2. MtPAR directly bound MtIFS1 and GmIFS2 promoters in yeast one-hybrid assays. 3. Repression of IFS was associated with reduced major isoflavonoids and redirected precursor flux toward PA production.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced isoflavone content accompanies increased PA flux
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper functionally emphasizes IFS repression as a mechanism of metabolic flux redistribution rather than direct engineering of IFS itself.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, ArrayExpress E-MEXP-3485; metabolomics by UPLC-ESI-qTOF-MS/LC-MS-MS

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Medicago truncatula; Glycine max; Arabidopsis thaliana; Medicago sativa
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Jemalong A17; Tianlong No.1; Col-0/tt2 mutant; RGSD4
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Hairy roots; seeds; alfalfa shoots/leaves and stems
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forage bloat protection and flavonoid defense-associated functions
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA biosynthesis; isoflavone branch repression; precursor flux partitioning between anthocyanin and PA branches
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional repression of IFS promoters and transcriptional activation of PA genes through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Central PA-promoting regulator that simultaneously represses isoflavone synthesis and redirects anthocyanidin precursor flux into the PA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MtIFS1; GmIFS2; ANR; UGT72L1; MtMATE1; downstream MBW-related genes MtEGL3; MtGL2; MtTT8; MtTTG2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses IFS and other isoflavone genes; activates PA-specific genes including ANR, UGT72L1 and MtMATE1; does not strongly induce anthocyanin-specific UGTs/MtMATE2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes PA accumulation, decreases isoflavones, and reduces anthocyanins by redirecting shared precursors; partially complements Arabidopsis tt2 PA-deficient seeds
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root and stable overexpression; Arabidopsis tt2 complementation; qRT-PCR/microarray; metabolite profiling; yeast one-hybrid; yeast two-hybrid; promoter transactivation assay; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtPAR overexpression increased soluble/insoluble PAs and reduced isoflavones in Medicago hairy roots. 2. MtPAR directly bound MtIFS1 and GmIFS2 promoters, repressing the isoflavone pathway. 3. MtPAR interacted with MtTT8 and MtWD40-1, and with soybean orthologs, to form an MBW complex that activated ANR. 4. MtPAR complemented the Arabidopsis tt2 mutant seed PA defect. 5. Co-expression with MtLAP1 additively increased PA production in alfalfa hairy roots and plants.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in transgenic hairy roots, Arabidopsis seeds, and alfalfa plants
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MtTT8; MtWD40-1; GmTT8a; GmTT8b; GmWD40a; GmWD40b
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms MYB-bHLH-WD40 ternary complex with MtTT8 and MtWD40-1; also interacts with soybean TT8/WD40 ortholog complexes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transgenic ectopic expression; co-expression with MtLAP1
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising regulator for engineering PA accumulation in forage legumes; best used in combination with MtLAP1 to increase precursor supply and achieve higher PA levels for bloat-safe alfalfa
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Decreases isoflavone content and can reduce anthocyanin accumulation when precursor supply is limiting
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts similarly to AtTT2 in PA regulation; may repress isoflavone biosynthesis without a canonical repressor domain, possibly by promoter competition or indirect mechanisms
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray, ArrayExpress E-MEXP-3485; metabolomics by UPLC-ESI-qTOF-MS/LC-MS-MS

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Forage bloat protection via elevated PA when combined with MtPAR
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis and anthocyanidin precursor supply to PA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthetic genes; additive cooperation with MtPAR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Provides anthocyanidin precursor supply that enables stronger MtPAR-driven PA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL; C4H; 4CL; CHS; F3H; ANS; ANR (in co-expression context)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin/flavonoid biosynthetic genes and, with MtPAR, additively upregulates PA and anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly increases anthocyanins alone and markedly enhances PA accumulation when co-expressed with MtPAR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy root overexpression; stable alfalfa co-expression lines; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtLAP1 overexpression in alfalfa hairy roots caused massive anthocyanin accumulation but only slight PA increase. 2. Co-expression of MtLAP1 with MtPAR significantly enhanced PA and anthocyanin accumulation. 3. Additive induction of flavonoid pathway genes supported the idea that increased anthocyanidin precursor supply improves PA engineering.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in transgenic alfalfa hairy roots and plants
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely functions in MBW complex with MtTT8 and MtWD40-1; discussed as possibly acting with MtPAR through shared TT8/WD40 partners
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transgenic ectopic expression; co-expression with MtPAR
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful partner gene to stack with MtPAR for high-PA alfalfa because it boosts anthocyanidin precursor pool
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong anthocyanin accumulation and possible flux diversion to anthocyanins when used alone
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not the main mechanistic focus, it is a core experimental gene in the engineering strategy section of the Results.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA gene activation through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required partner enabling MtPAR-mediated transactivation of ANR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enables activation of ANR when combined with MtPAR and MtWD40-1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports PA biosynthesis as a complex component; without the full complex, strong ANR activation was not observed
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; transient promoter transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtTT8 interacted with MtPAR in yeast. 2. MtTT8 together with MtPAR and MtWD40-1 activated the ANR promoter strongly. 3. MtTT8 transcript was upregulated in MtPAR-overexpressing roots.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in MtPAR-overexpressing Medicago hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MtPAR overexpression correlates with higher transcript abundance
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential supporting factor for PA engineering, though not directly engineered alone in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core mechanistic partner rather than primary engineering target

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA gene activation through MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required MBW component for MtPAR/MtTT8-dependent activation of ANR
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports strong ANR activation when combined with MtPAR and MtTT8
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a necessary cofactor for PA pathway activation by MtPAR complex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid; transient promoter transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MtWD40-1 interacted with MtPAR in yeast. 2. MtPAR, MtTT8 and MtWD40-1 together strongly activated the ANR promoter. 3. Overexpression of MtWD40-1 alone had previously failed to increase PA, indicating context-dependent function as complex partner.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supportive complex component for PA engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Scaffold factor needed for complex assembly rather than stand-alone determinant
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Yeast interaction assays; transient transactivation assay
